UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores profile information and subscription details for a user of the CareerCraft AI application. This serves as the core identity for managing user-specific data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and notifications.

plan.description: The current subscription plan of the user (e.g., 'free', 'premium', 'enterprise').

onboardingStep.description: Tracks the user's progress through the initial application onboarding flow.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the user profile.

Resume.title: Resume
Resume.description: Represents a user's resume document, which can be a comprehensive 'Master Resume' or a 'Targeted Resume' adapted for a specific job.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Resume entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this resume. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Resume)

title.description: A descriptive title for the resume (e.g., 'Master Resume', 'Software Engineer for Google').

isMasterResume.description: Indicates if this resume is the user's primary, comprehensive master resume.

masterResumeId.description: Reference to the Master Resume from which this targeted resume was derived. Only applicable if 'isMasterResume' is false. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Resume)

jobSpecId.description: Reference to the JobSpecification entity for which this resume was tailored. Only applicable if 'isMasterResume' is false. (Relationship: JobSpecification 1:N Resume)

sourceFileId.description: Reference to the UploadedFile entity from which this resume was initially built or uploaded. (Relationship: UploadedFile 1:N Resume)

summary.description: The AI-generated or user-provided professional summary or objective for the resume.

workExperienceIds.description: References to WorkExperience entities associated with this resume, ordered by importance or date. (Relationship: Resume 1:N WorkExperience)

educationIds.description: References to Education entities associated with this resume, ordered by importance or date. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Education)

skillIds.description: References to Skill entities associated with this resume, representing expertise areas. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Skill)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the resume was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last modification to the resume.

WorkExperience.title: WorkExperience
WorkExperience.description: Represents a single work experience entry on a resume, detailing a past or current job role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkExperience entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume entity this work experience belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N WorkExperience)

title.description: The job title held (e.g., 'Senior Software Engineer').

companyName.description: The name of the company where the experience was gained.

location.description: The city and state/country of the employment.

startDate.description: The start date of the employment.

endDate.description: The end date of the employment. This field is null if 'isCurrent' is true.

isCurrent.description: Indicates if this is a current job role.

description.description: A detailed description of responsibilities and key achievements in the role.

Education.title: Education
Education.description: Represents a single education entry on a resume, detailing academic achievements.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Education entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume entity this education entry belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Education)

institutionName.description: The name of the educational institution.

degree.description: The degree or qualification obtained (e.g., 'Bachelor of Science', 'Master of Arts').

fieldOfStudy.description: The specific field or major of study (e.g., 'Computer Science', 'Business Administration').

startDate.description: The start date of the education period.

endDate.description: The end date of the education period.

grade.description: Optional: GPA, honors, or other academic distinctions (e.g., '3.8 GPA', 'Magna Cum Laude').

description.description: Optional: Relevant coursework, academic projects, or activities.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a specific skill or area of expertise relevant to a resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume entity this skill is associated with. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Skill)

name.description: The name of the skill (e.g., 'Python', 'Project Management', 'Data Analysis').

category.description: Optional: A category for the skill (e.g., 'Programming Languages', 'Soft Skills', 'Tools').

proficiency.description: Optional: The user's proficiency level with the skill (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced', 'Expert').

UploadedFile.title: UploadedFile
UploadedFile.description: Stores metadata about files (e.g., resumes, job specifications) uploaded by users for processing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UploadedFile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded this file. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UploadedFile)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileType.description: The MIME type of the file (e.g., 'application/pdf', 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document').

storagePath.description: The path or URL to the file's location in cloud storage (e.g., Firebase Storage).

contentType.description: Specifies what the uploaded file represents (e.g., 'resume', 'jobSpec').

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the file was uploaded.

originalContent.description: The extracted plain text content from the file, or the URL if it was a web link upload.

JobSpecification.title: JobSpecification
JobSpecification.description: Stores the details of a job specification provided by the user, used for tailoring resumes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobSpecification entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who provided this job specification. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N JobSpecification)

title.description: The job title extracted from the specification (e.g., 'Senior Software Engineer').

companyName.description: The company name associated with the job specification.

description.description: The full text content of the job description.

sourceUrl.description: The original URL if the job specification was provided via a web link.

associatedFileId.description: Reference to the UploadedFile entity if the job spec was from an uploaded document. (Relationship: UploadedFile 1:N JobSpecification)

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the job specification was provided or uploaded.

lastAnalyzedAt.description: Timestamp of the last AI analysis performed on this job specification.

AIRequest.title: AIRequest
AIRequest.description: Logs details of AI-powered processing requests made by users, such as resume suggestions or job matching analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIRequest entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the AI request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N AIRequest)

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume entity if the request is related to a specific resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N AIRequest)

jobSpecId.description: Reference to the JobSpecification entity if the request is related to a specific job specification. (Relationship: JobSpecification 1:N AIRequest)

inputData.description: The input parameters or data sent to the AI model, stored as a JSON string.

result.description: The structured output or response received from the AI model, stored as a JSON string.

status.description: Current status of the AI request (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'failed').

errorMessage.description: Details if the AI request failed.

requestedAt.description: Timestamp when the AI request was initiated.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the AI request was completed or failed.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records payment transactions made by users for plan upgrades or subscriptions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the payment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Payment)

transactionId.description: The unique transaction ID from the payment gateway (e.g., Stripe).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the payment record.

OnlineResume.title: OnlineResume
OnlineResume.description: Represents a publicly shareable web page version of a user's resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OnlineResume entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this online resume. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N OnlineResume)

resumeId.description: Reference to the specific Resume entity this online version is based on. (Relationship: Resume 1:1 OnlineResume)

publicUrlSlug.description: A unique, user-friendly slug used to form the public URL for accessing this online resume.

isPublished.description: Indicates whether the online resume is currently live and publicly accessible.

themeSettings.description: JSON string representing customization options for the online resume's appearance (e.g., colors, fonts, layout).

viewCount.description: A counter for how many times the online resume has been viewed.

publishedAt.description: Timestamp when the online resume was first published.

lastUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the online resume's content or settings.

LinkTree.title: LinkTree
LinkTree.description: Represents a user's customizable 'link in bio' style page, consolidating various online profiles and resources.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LinkTree entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this LinkTree. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N LinkTree)

publicUrlSlug.description: A unique, user-friendly slug used to form the public URL for accessing this LinkTree.

isPublished.description: Indicates whether the LinkTree is currently live and publicly accessible.

profileTitle.description: The main title displayed on the LinkTree page (e.g., 'John Doe | My Professional Links').

bio.description: A short descriptive text or personal statement for the LinkTree page.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture displayed on the LinkTree.

links.description: An array of JSON strings, where each string represents a link object (e.g., '{"title": "LinkedIn", "url": "https://linkedin.com/in/johndoe", "icon": "linkedin"}').

themeSettings.description: JSON string representing customization options for the LinkTree's appearance (e.g., colors, fonts, layout).

viewCount.description: A counter for how many times the LinkTree page has been viewed.

publishedAt.description: Timestamp when the LinkTree was first published.

lastUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the LinkTree's content or settings.

definition.description: Stores profile information and subscription details for a user. This document is directly owned by the authenticated user, where '{userId}' matches 'request.auth.uid'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, typically corresponding to Firebase Authentication's UID.

definition.description: Stores a user's resume document (master or targeted). Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring '{userId}' in the path matches 'resource.data.userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific resume.

definition.description: Stores a single work experience entry belonging to a specific resume. Ownership is tied to the parent resume's 'userId', which can be derived from the path (i.e., '{userId}' must match 'request.auth.uid').

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent resume.

params.name: workExperienceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the work experience entry.

definition.description: Stores a single education entry belonging to a specific resume. Ownership is tied to the parent resume's 'userId', which can be derived from the path (i.e., '{userId}' must match 'request.auth.uid').

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent resume.

params.name: educationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the education entry.

definition.description: Stores a single skill entry belonging to a specific resume. Ownership is tied to the parent resume's 'userId', which can be derived from the path (i.e., '{userId}' must match 'request.auth.uid').

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent resume.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier for the skill entry.

definition.description: Stores metadata about files uploaded by users. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring '{userId}' in the path matches 'resource.data.userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who uploaded the file.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded file.

definition.description: Stores details of a job specification provided by the user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring '{userId}' in the path matches 'resource.data.userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who provided the job specification.

params.name: jobSpecId
params.description: The unique identifier for the job specification.

definition.description: Logs details of AI-powered processing requests made by users. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring '{userId}' in the path matches 'resource.data.userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated the AI request.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the AI request.

definition.description: Records payment transactions made by users. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring '{userId}' in the path matches 'resource.data.userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who made the payment.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the payment transaction.

definition.description: Stores a user's private settings and metadata for an online resume. This is the user-editable version. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring '{userId}' in the path matches 'resource.data.userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this online resume.

params.name: onlineResumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the online resume.

definition.description: Stores publicly accessible online resume data, denormalized from the user's private online resume when 'isPublished' is true. Access is public read if 'resource.data.isPublished' is true, and owner-only write. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'isPublished' for authorization and QAPs.

params.name: onlineResumePublicSlug
params.description: A unique, user-friendly slug used to form the public URL for accessing this online resume.

definition.description: Stores a user's private settings and metadata for a LinkTree. This is the user-editable version. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring '{userId}' in the path matches 'resource.data.userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this LinkTree.

params.name: linkTreeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the LinkTree.

definition.description: Stores publicly accessible LinkTree data, denormalized from the user's private LinkTree when 'isPublished' is true. Access is public read if 'resource.data.isPublished' is true, and owner-only write. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'isPublished' for authorization and QAPs.

params.name: linkTreePublicSlug
params.description: A unique, user-friendly slug used to form the public URL for accessing this LinkTree.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling. Each user's data is primarily stored within a dedicated subcollection under their unique user ID, like `/users/{userId}/...`. This ensures strong data isolation and simplifies security rules, adhering to the path-based ownership model.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
For every document that belongs to a user (e.g., Resume, UploadedFile, JobSpecification, AIRequest, Payment, private OnlineResume, private LinkTree), the `userId` is explicitly included in the document's data. This allows security rules to directly evaluate `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents or parse complex path segments. This is crucial for enabling atomic operations (transactions, batches) and making rules simple to debug. Even for deeply nested entities like WorkExperience, Education, and Skill, while their `userId` can be inferred from the path `/users/{userId}/resumes/{resumeId}/...`, including `userId` directly in the document further enhances this independence and simplifies rules.

**Structural Segregation for QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
To address the requirement for public sharing and easy querying (QAPs), entities like `OnlineResume` and `LinkTree` are segregated into two distinct collection structures:
1.  **Private/Owned Data:** Stored under `/users/{userId}/onlineResumes/{onlineResumeId}` and `/users/{userId}/linkTrees/{linkTreeId}`. These paths grant the owner full CRUD access.
2.  **Public Data:** Stored under `/onlineResumesPublic/{onlineResumePublicSlug}` and `/linkTreesPublic/{linkTreePublicSlug}`. These collections contain denormalized, publicly relevant subsets of the data. Each document in these public collections explicitly includes the `userId` of the owner and an `isPublished` flag. This allows for straightforward security rules such as `allow read: if resource.data.isPublished == true;` and `allow write: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;`. This segregation ensures that `list` operations on the public collections only return genuinely public items, eliminating the need for client-side filtering and making rules robust.

**Company Multitenancy, User Roles, and Plan-based Views:**
*   **Multitenancy:** Achieved implicitly through `userId` ownership. Each user acts as a 'tenant' of their own data, which is securely isolated by the `/users/{userId}` path.
*   **User Roles (DBAC):** While explicit 'roles' collections (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`) are not present in the provided schema, any such roles would be stored in a dedicated top-level collection, with authorization based on `request.auth.uid`'s existence within that role collection. For the current setup, user ownership is the primary 'role'.
*   **Plan-based Control:** The `UserProfile` entity contains a `plan` field. For security rules that depend on a user's plan (e.g., feature limits, access to premium content), the `plan` would ideally be denormalized into the specific documents whose access is conditional on it, or plan enforcement would occur via Cloud Functions (which is generally more robust for write operations/limits). For read access, a `get()` call to the user's profile to check their plan might be used if denormalization is not feasible, but it impacts atomic operations. The structure allows for rules to check `request.auth.uid` against the document's `userId` first, and then potentially `resource.data.plan` if denormalized, or use a `get()` call to `/users/$(request.auth.uid)` within the rules if absolutely necessary, but this is a less preferred approach for authorization independence.
